title: erigeron × schelkovnikovii
intro: Erigeron × schelkovnikovii, also known as Schelkownikow's daisy, is a rare and hybrid species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in folk medicine, possessing medicinal properties attributed to their high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Erigeron × schelkovnikovii include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, although its availability and cultivation are limited due to its rare and limited geographical distribution.
